observations.recommendation: 23. We recommended that concerned municipal officials and employees, who are determined as persons responsible/liable, settle/comply immediately with the requirements of the subject of disallowances.

1.29. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Inventory
Committee to continue the physical count of all PPE items taking into
consideration the prescribed procedural guidelines for inventory
taking as provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006, to wit:
a. Prepare an approved Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) prior to
the physical count in order to ensure that all planned inventory
activities/procedures are efficiently and effectively performed
by the Committee members;
b. Use the inventory working papers provided by the General
Service Office (GSO) as basis for inventory taking activities in
considering the capitalization threshold of ₱50,000.00;
c. Record/document the daily physical count in a standard
Inventory Count Form (ICF), which shall be used as the basis
in the preparation of the RPCPPE after the physical count;
d. Tag all PPE items counted with new property stickers
containing the information provided by the said Circular;
e. State clearly in the ICF the PPEs’ condition, whether found at
station or not during the physical count which may be
described as “non-existing” or “missing”, as well as other
relevant information of each PPE; and
f. Prepare the RPCPPE by equipment type upon completion of
the physical count with complete data and information of the
property items.

1.30. We recommended further that Local Chief Executive require the:
a. The Municipal Accountant to compute the depreciation of the
affected PPE accounts and then recognize and record in the
books of accounts by drawing a journal entry voucher (JEV)
and update/post the same in its corresponding Journals and
Ledgers; and
b. The Municipal Accountant to maintain and update the
Property, Plant and Equipment Ledger Cards (PPELC) for
each category of assets and Real Property Ledger Cards
(RPLC) for Land for all properties acquired and purchased;
c. The Local GSO to maintain Property Cards (PC) per category
of property, plant and equipment for all properties received
and under his accountability.

2.6. We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:
a. Review the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant
and Equipment (RPCPPE) as of December 31, 2022 prepared
and submitted by Municipal General Service Officer;
b. Take note and reclassify tangible items that were acquired
prior to CY 2022 with a cost of ₱50,000.00 and below to semi-
expendable property by drawing a Journal Entry Voucher
(JEV) through debiting/crediting the Prior Period Adjustment
and Government Equity accounts and the corresponding
accumulated depreciation and accumulated impairment loss
accounts in the books of accounts:
c. Retain the existing Property Acknowledgement Receipts
(PARs) issued to end-users which shall serve as the Inventory
Custodian Slip (ICS) until their accountabilities for such items
are extinguished; and
d. Issue the corresponding Inventory Custodian Slip (ICS) for all
semi-expendable property is acquired in the ensuing year.

3.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Local
Treasurer to promptly make the request for bank statements at the
end of the month and follow-up as frequent as possible to initiate the
preparation of the BRS.

3.7 We also reiterated our recommendation that the Local Chief
Executive direct the Local Accountant to promptly prepare and
submit the Bank Reconciliation Statements (BRSs) immediately upon
receipt of the bank statements from the authorized government
depository banks (AGDBs) for all bank accounts presenting complete
reconciling items such as outstanding checks/cancelled checks deposit
in transit/unrecorded deposits, bank charges/errors, if any.

4.6. We recommended that the LGU Accountant conduct a regular and
periodic verification, analysis, and validation of the existence of those
dormant accounts and make the necessary adjusting entry/ies in the
books of accounts, if any, as required in Item No. 7 of COA Circular
No. 2016-005 and Item No. III.B of COA Circular No. 97-001.

4.7. We further recommended that the LGU Local Chief Executive file the
request for authority to write-off the dormant accounts to the Office
of the Audit Team Leaders (ATL) and/or Supervising Auditor (SA), if
warranted, following the procedures prescribed in Item No. 8 of COA
Circular No. 2016-005 and Item No. III.A.10 of COA Circular No. 97-
001.

5.9. We recommended that the Management establish a proper, efficient,
and effective administration of real property tax through the
following courses of actions:
For Local Assessor
a. Perform the regular appraisal and assessment of all real
property situated in his locality as provided in RA 7160 on the
rules and regulations for the classification, appraisal, and
assessment of real property; and
b. Prepare, maintain and submit the Assessment Roll to the Local
Treasurer on or before the thirty-first (31st) day of December
each year.
For Local Treasurer
a. Furnish a duly certified list showing the name of taxpayers and
the amount due and collectible for the year to the Local
Accountant;
b. Post on or before the thirty-first (31st) day of January each
year the notice of the dates when tax may be paid without
interest at a conspicuous and publicly accessible place at the
municipal hall; and
c. Collect the real property tax in full amount or by four
installment bases.
For Local Accountant
a. At the beginning of the year, draw a Journal Entry Voucher
(JEV) based on the list coming from Local Treasurer to record
the debit to Real Property Tax Receivable and crediting to
Deferred Real Property Tax Income;
b. Upon collection of Real Property Taxes from taxpayers, debit
the account Deferred Real Property Tax Income while credit
the accounts Real Property Tax Income for Municipal share
and Due to LGUs for Province and Barangay shares; and
c. Prepare and furnish the Provincial Accountant a summary of
the JEV showing the breakdown of the amounts Due to LGUs
and the Abstract of Real Property Tax to facilitate the
distribution of real property tax collection.

6.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Treasurer
to observe the timely remittance of taxes withheld from
employees/suppliers and of premium contributions of both employees
and employer to the concerned Offices, as the case maybe, as
prescribed by BIR, GSIS, Pag-ibig and PhilHealth in order to avoid
possible penalties.

1. Prepare the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) prior to its inventory taking in order to ensure that all planned inventory activities/procedures are efficiently and effectively performed by the Committee members
2. Use the inventory working papers provided by the General Service Office (GSO) as basis for inventory taking activities considering the capitalization threshold of ₱15,000.00;
3. Record/document the daily physical count in a standard Inventory Count Form (ICF), which shall be used as the basis in the preparation of the RPCPPE after the physical count;
4. Tag all PPE items counted with new property stickers containing the information provided by the said Circular

5. State clearly in the ICF the PPEs’ condition, whether found at station or not during the physical count which may be described as “non-existing” or “missing”, as well as other relevant information of each PPE; and
6. Prepare the RPCPPE upon completion of the physical count.

7. We recommended further that Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to compute the depreciation of the affected PPE accounts and then recognize and record in the books of accounts by drawing a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) and update/post the same in its corresponding Journals and Ledgers.
8. Furnish the Local Accountant with the Bank Statements (BS) within five (5) days after the end of each month
9. Furnish the Auditor the balances, details and bank statements of the cash account balances maintained by the LGU; and
10. Promptly prepare and submit the Bank Reconciliation Statement upon receipt of the bank statements from the AGDBs
11. Take up in the books of account any reconciling item noted.
12. Bank Reconciliation Statements – within ten (10) days from receipts of the Bank Statements furnished by the LGU depository
13. Report of Checks Issued, Report of Disbursements, and Report of Collections and Deposits – immediately after recorded in the books of accounts, within ten (10) days of the ensuing month;
14. Pre-closing Trial Balance – not later than the twentieth day after the end of the month
15. Post-closing Trial Balance – not later than the fourteenth day of February after the end of the calendar year
16. Year-end Financial Statements – within 60 days after 31st of December of each year
17. Consolidated Report of Accountability for Accountable Forms (CRAAF) – not later than the fifth day of the ensuing month
18. Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (DRRMF) – on or before 15th day after the end of each month.

19. We recommended further that the Local Chief Executive requires the LGU authorized government depository bank to furnish a monthly reports on the condition of the LGU account standing on their books in pursuant to Section 74 of PD 1445.
20. Make use of the prescribed format provided by the NGAS Manual for LGU, Volumes I and II;
21. Provide all the necessary information and data in the financial reports/statement such as detail description, unit measure, report number, and/or remarks; and
22. Reproduce the required number of copies for proper distribution to concerned offices.
24. Direct the LGU Accountant to prepare a lapsed schedule for the account, computation of the depreciation for each component of Road Network account at the end of the year; and
25. Direct the LGU Accountant also to take the necessary adjustments noted in the books of accounts by drawing a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV)

26. Direct the Local Inventory Committee to continue the conduct of a physical inventory of all equipment belonging to their respective offices; and (2) then to submit a complete Annual Physical Inventory of all property under the agency control as of 31, December, on General Form (41) not later than 31 January of each year;
27. Require the LGU General Services Officer to maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map

28. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive compel those responsible local officers to act and do their respective duties and functions faithfully in accordance with laws, rules and regulations as well as adherence in the application of internal control measures to ensure that all moneys and properties of the Municipality are properly accounted for and taken up in the books of accounts.

We recommended that PPE Inventory Committee to prepare the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) prior to its inventory taking in order to ensure that all planned inventory activities/procedures are efficiently and effectively performed by the Committee members.

We recommended that Management create a separate special account in the General Fund (SAGF) for the BGCM Fund through an ordinance by the local sangguniang pursuant to Section 313 of RA No. 7160 for purposes of easy monitoring/tracking of the utilization of the allocation thereof.
We recommended that concerned municipal officials and employees, who are determined as persons responsible/liable, settle/comply immediately with the requirements of the subject of disallowances.

1.17. We recommended that Inventory Committee continue the physical
count of all PPE items taking into consideration the prescribed
procedural guidelines for inventory taking as provided in COA
Circular No. 2020-006, to wit:
a) Prepare the Physical Inventory Plan (PIP) prior to its
inventory taking in order to ensure that all planned inventory
activities/procedures are efficiently and effectively performed
by the Committee members.
b) Use the inventory working papers provided by the General
Service Office (GSO) as basis for inventory taking activities
considering the capitalization threshold of ₱15,000.00;
c) Record/document the daily physical count in a standard
Inventory Count Form (ICF), which shall be used as the basis
in the preparation of the RPCPPE after the physical count;
d) Tag all PPE items counted with new property stickers
containing the information provided by the said Circular;
e) State clearly in the ICF the PPEs’ condition, whether found at
station or not during the physical count which may be
described as “non-existing” or “missing”, as well as other
relevant information of each PPE; and
f) Prepare the RPCPPE upon completion of the physical count.

1.18. We recommended further that Local Chief Executive require the
Municipal Accountant to compute the depreciation of the affected
PPE accounts and then recognize and record in the books of accounts
by drawing a journal entry voucher (JEV) and update/post the same
in its corresponding Journals and Ledgers.

2.9 We recommended that Local Chief Executive:
a) Direct the authorized government depository banks (AGDBs)
to: a) furnish the Local Accountant with the Bank Statements
(BS) within five (5) days after the end of each month; and b)
furnish the Auditor the balances, details and bank statements
of the cash account balances maintained by the LGU; and
b) Require the Local Accountant to: a) promptly prepare and
submit the Bank Reconciliation Statement upon receipt of the
bank statements from the AGDBs; and b) take up in the books
of account any reconciling item noted.

3.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the concerned
local officials and accountable officers to submit the following
accounting records, reports and forms to the Auditor within the
reglementary period as prescribed by the pertinent applicable COA
rules and regulations, to wit:

a) For local Accountant
1) Bank Reconciliation Statements – within ten (10) days
from receipts of the Bank Statements furnished by the
LGU depository; and
2) Report of Checks Issued, Report of Disbursements, and
Report of Collections and Deposits – immediately after
recorded in the books of accounts, within ten (10) days
of the ensuing month;
3) Pre-closing Trial Balance – not later than the twentieth
day after the end of the month;
4) Post-closing Trial Balance – not later than the
fourteenth day of February after the end of the
calendar year; and
5) Year-end Financial Statements – within 60 days after
31st of December of each year;

b) For local Treasurer, consolidated Report of Accountability for
Accountable Forms (CRAAF) – not later than the fifth day of
the ensuing month; and

c) For local DRRM Officer, report on Sources and Utilization of
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (DRRMF) –
on or before 15th day after the end of each month.

3.8 We recommended further that the Local Chief Executive requires the
LGU authorized government depository bank to furnish a monthly
reports on the condition of the LGU account standing on their books
in pursuant to Section 74 of PD 1445.

4.7 We recommended that the concerned local Accountant Officers
faithfully observed the following courses of actions in the preparation
and submission of their financial reports and statements, such as:

a) Make use of the prescribed format provided by the NGAS
Manual for LGU, Volumes I and II;
b) Provide all the necessary information and data in the financial
reports/statement such as detail description, unit measure,
report number, and/or remarks; and
c) Reproduce the required number of copies for proper
distribution to concerned offices.

5.4 We recommended that concerned municipal officials and employees,
who are determined as persons responsible/liable, settle/comply
immediately with the requirements of the subject of disallowances.

We recommended that the Inventory Committee continue the physical count of all PPE items taking into consideration the prescribed procedural guidelines for inventory taking as provided in COA Circular No. 2020-006, to wit: 1. Use the inventory working papers provided by the General Service Office (GSO) as basis for inventory taking activities, taking into consideration the capitalization threshold of ₱15,000.00; 2. Record/document the daily physical count in a standard Inventory Count Form (ICF), which shall be used as the basis in the preparation of the RPCPPE after the physical count; 3. Tag all PPE items counted with new property stickers containing the information provided by the said Circular; 4. State clearly in the ICF the PPEs’ condition, found at station and not found during the physical count which described as “non-existing” or “missing”, as well as other relevant information of each PPE; and 5. Prepare the RPCPPE upon completion of the physical count.

We recommended that Management require the accountant to compute, recognize and record in the books of accounts the depreciation of the affected PPE accounts by drawing a journal voucher and update/post the same in its corresponding Journals and Ledgers.

We recommended that Management prepare: 9. A monthly report on BGCM fund utilization and status of implementation of PPAs in pursuant to DBM Local Budget Circular No. 125; and 10. A separate post-closing trial balance and financial statements for BGCM Fund as special account for General Fund, which contains the summary of transactions for the year as integral part of the schedules of the General Fund trial balance for purposes of proper recording in the books of accounts in pursuant to Section 110 of NGAS Manual for LGUs, Volume I.

We recommended that the following courses of actions be made by the Local Chief Executive, to wit: a) Direct the LGU Accountant: 12. To conduct depth review at least at each annual financial reporting date the residual value and the useful life of an asset upon recognition of depreciable amount of each lug’s property, plant and equipment; 13. To prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component of Road Network account at the end of the year; 14. To take the necessary adjustments noted in the books of accounts by drawing a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV); and 15. Direct the Local Inventory Committee to continue the conduct of a physical inventory of all equipment belonging to their respective offices; and (2) then to submit a complete Annual Physical Inventory of all property under the agency control as of 31, December, on General Form (41) not later than 31 January of each year; 16. Require the LGU General Services Officer to maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map; and 17. Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the local accountant and the general services officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive compel those responsible local officers to act and do their respective duties and functions faithfully in accordance with laws, rules and regulations as well as adherence in the application of internal control measures to ensure that all moneys and properties of the Municipality are properly accounted for and taken up in the books of accounts.
We recommended that Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Accountant to disclose all unobligated balances of local DRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements using the prescribed format.

We recommended that the LGU Local Chief Executive direct the LGU Accountant to secure the monthly bank statement account from its authorized government depository bank (AGDB) for all their bank accounts maintained and then prepare the corresponding monthly bank reconciliation statements for each bank account in accordance with Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and Sections 3.1 and 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011.

1.10 We recommended that the Inventory Committee continue the physical
count of all PPE items taking into consideration the prescribed
procedural guidelines for inventory taking as provided in COA
Circular No. 2020-006, to wit:

a. Use the inventory working papers provided by the General Service
Office (GSO) as basis for inventory taking activities, taking into
consideration the capitalization threshold of ₱15,000.00;
b. Record/document the daily physical count in a standard Inventory
Count Form (ICF), which shall be used as the basis in the
preparation of the RPCPPE after the physical count;
c. Tag all PPE items counted with new property stickers containing the
information provided by the said Circular;
d. State clearly in the ICF the PPEs’ condition, found at station and
not found during the physical count which described as “non-existing” or “missing”, as well as other relevant information of each
PPE; and
e. Prepare the RPCPPE upon completion of the physical count.

2.7 We recommended that Management require the accountant to
compute, recognize and record in the books of accounts the
depreciation of the affected PPE accounts by drawing a journal voucher
and update/post the same in its corresponding Journals and Ledgers.

3.5 We recommended that Inventory Committee shall prepare the Physical
Inventory Plan (PIP) prior to its inventory taking in order to ensure
that all planned inventory activities/procedures are efficiently and
effectively performed by the Committee members.

4.7 We recommended that Management create a separate special account
in the General Fund (SAGF) for the BGCM Fund through an
ordinance by the local Sangguniang pursuant to Section 313 of RA No.
7160 for purposes of easy monitoring/tracking of the utilization of the
allocation thereof.

5.9 We recommended that Management prepare:

a. A monthly report on BGCM fund utilization and status of
implementation of PPAs in pursuant to DBM Local Budget Circular
No. 125; and
b. A separate post-closing trial balance and financial statements for
BGCM fund as special account for General Fund, which contains
the summary of transactions for the year as integral part of the
schedules of the General Fund trial balance for purposes of proper
recording in the books of accounts in pursuant to Section 110,
Volume I of NGAS Manual for LGUs.

6.3 We recommended that concerned municipal officials and employees,
who are determined as persons responsible/liable, settle/comply
immediately with the requirements of the subject of disallowances.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive compel those responsible municipal officers to act and do their respective duties and functions faithfully in accordance with laws, rules and regulations as well as adherence in the application of internal control measures to ensure that all moneys and properties of the Municipality are properly accounted for and taken up in the books of accounts.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive:
10. Require the local Accountant to review all the taxes withheld from all local officials’ compensation and from all suppliers for goods purchased or services rendered; and
11. Encourage the local Treasurer to continue remit the taxes withheld on individual employee/supplier and mandatory premium contributions of employee/employer to the concerned Offices, as the case maybe, on or before the due date manner of prescribed by BIR, GSIS, Pag-ibig and Philhealth Offices.

We recommended that concerned LBO attend trainings on the BOM for LGUs. And further, require him and all other concerned municipal officials to make use of the prescribed forms and/or contents of the budgetary documents in the preparation of municipal annual budget in compliance with LBC No. 112 and BOM for LGUs.

We recommended that management instruct:
13. The members of the LDRRMC to include in the Local Disaster Risk Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) all unexpended balances of local DRRMF; and
14. The Municipal Accountant to disclose all unobligated balances of local DRRMF in the Notes to the Financial Statements using the prescribed format.

We recommended that the LGU Local Chief Executive direct the LGU Accountant to secure the monthly bank statement from its authorized government depository bank (AGDB) for all their bank accounts maintained and then prepare the corresponding monthly bank reconciliation statements for each bank account in accordance with Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and Sections 3.1 and 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011.

We recommended that top management instruct:
16. The Municipal Treasurer to issue a check with complete supporting documents for the fund transfer of unexpended LDRRM Fund from General Fund to Special Trust Fund, and The Municipal Accountant to record the transfer of unexpended LDRRM Fund by drawing a Journal Entry Voucher taking the appropriate accounting entries under General and Trust Fund.
17. For General Fund books, debit the account Transfer of Unspent Current Year DRRM Funds to the Trust Funds (Account Code 5-02-15-010) and credit the account Cash in Bank – Local Currency Current Account (Account Code 1-01-02-010).
18. While for Trust Fund books, debit the account Cash in Bank – Local Currency Current Account (Account Code 1-01-02-010) and credit the account Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (Account Code 2-04-01-020).

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should direct the Municipal Accountant to: a. Conduct depth review at least at each annual reporting date the residual value and the useful life of an asset upon recognition of depreciable amount of each lug’s property, plant and equipment; b. Take the necessary adjustments noted in the books of accounts by drawing a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV); and c. If expectations differ from previous estimates, the change(s) is accounted for as a change in an accounting estimate in accordance with IPSAS 3, Accounting Policies, Changes in Accounting Estimates and Errors.
observations.reason: due to incomplete source of information of PPE records.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that top management instruct: a. The Municipal Treasurer to issue a check with complete supporting documents for the fund transfer of unexpended LDRRM Fund from General Fund to Special Trust Fund, and b. The Municipal Account to record the transfer of unexpended LDRRM Fund by drawing a Journal Entry Voucher taking the appropriate accounting entries under General and Trust Fund. i. For General Fund books, debit the account Transfer of Unspent Current Year DRRM Funds to the Trust Funds (Account Code 5-02-15-010) and credit the account Cash in Bank – Local Currency Current Account (Account Code 1-01-02-010). ii. While for Trust Fund books, debit the account Cash in Bank – Local Currency Current Account (Account Code 1-01-02-010) and credit the account Trust Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (Account Code 2-04-01-020).

We recommended that direct the Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Officer (LDDRMO) to prepare and submit Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) based on the LDRRM Plan and Budget approved by the LDDRM Council.
We recommended that the concerned municipal officials and employees who are determined as persons responsible/liable settle/comply immediately with the requirements of the subject of NS/ND/NC.

12. We recommended that the following courses of actions be made by the Local
Chief Executive, to wit:
a. Direct the LGU Accountant:
1. To secure the monthly bank statement from its authorized
government depository bank (AGDB) for all their bank accounts
maintained and then prepare the corresponding monthly bank
reconciliation statements for each bank accounts maintained in
accordance with Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and Section 3.2 of COA
Circular No. 96-011;
2. To conduct depth review at least at each annual reporting date the
residual value and the useful life of an asset upon recognition of
depreciable amount of each lug’s property, plant and equipment;
3. To prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the
depreciation for each component of Road Network account at the end
of the year;
4. To maintain a subsidiary record particularly Property Ledger Cards
(PLC) for property, plant and equipment accounts and Local Road
Network Ledger Card for local road network and its components,
and
5. To take the necessary adjustments noted in the books of accounts by
drawing a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV); and
b. Direct the Local Inventory Committee to continue the conduct of a
physical inventory of all equipment belonging to their respective offices;
and (2) then to submit a complete Annual Physical Inventory of all
property under the agency control as of 31, December, on General Form
(41) not later than 31 January of each year;

c. Require the LGU General Services Officer to maintain a Local Road
Inventory and Road Map; and
d. Direct the Municipal Engineer to provide the local accountant and the
general services officer with the complete description and cost
segregation of road components for road projects.

22. We recommended that Local Chief Executive compel those responsible local
officers to act and do their respective duties and functions faithfully in
accordance with laws, rules and regulations as well as adherence in the
application of internal control measures to ensure that all moneys and
properties of the Municipality are properly accounted for and taken up in the books of accounts.

33. We recommended that Local Chief Executive:
a. Require the local Accountant to review all the taxes withheld from all
local officials’ compensation and from all suppliers for goods purchased
or services rendered; and
b. Encourage the local Treasurer to continue remit the taxes withheld on
individual employee/supplier and mandatory premium contributions of
employee/employer to the concerned Offices, as the case maybe, on or
before the due date prescribed by BIR, GSIS, Pag-ibig and Philhealth
Offices.

40. We recommended that concerned LBO attend trainings on the BOM for LGUs.
And further, require him and all other concerned municipal officials to make
use of the prescribed forms and/or contents of the budgetary documents in the
preparation of municipal annual budget in compliance with LBC No. 112 and
BOM for LGUs.

46. We recommended that Local Chief Executive require:
a) The members of the LDRRMC to include in the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) all
unexpended balances of local DRRMF; and
b) The Municipal Accountant to disclose all unobligated balances of local
DRRMF in the Notes to Financial Statements using the prescribed format.

We recommended that the LGU Local Chief Executive direct the LGU Accountant to secure the monthly bank statement from its authorized government depository bank (AGDB) for all their bank accounts maintained and then prepare the corresponding monthly bank reconciliation statements for each bank accounts maintained in accordance with Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and Sections 3.1 and 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive to: a. Direct the Local Inventory Committee to continue the conduct of a physical inventory of all equipment belonging to their respective offices; and (2) then to submit a complete Annual Physical Inventory of all property under the agency control as of 31, December, on General Form (41) not later than 31 January of each year; and b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain a subsidiary record particularly Property Ledger Cards (PLC) for property, plant and equipment accounts.
observations.reason: due to laxity of Inventory Committee

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should enjoin the concerned local officials, to wit: a. Municipal Accountant i. Prepare the Journal Voucher to record the beginning balance of the local road network and its components in the general ledger and the Local Road Network Ledger Card, respectively; ii. Support the JV with the Registry of Public Infrastructures for Roads, working paper on the distribution of costs for the road components, and working paper on the determination of the depreciated replacement cost for road components with no available cost per registry; iii. Keep and maintain subsidiary records to roads and road components for every road network; and iv. Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation for each component at the end of the year. b. General Services Officer i. Maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map; and ii. Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card for all roads and its Components. c. Municipal Engineer i. Provide the local accountant and the general services officer with the complete description and cost segregation of road components for road projects.
observations.reason: due to compliance with the unavailability of some records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that LGU Management undertake the following course of actions in pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005, to wit:
a. Direct the LGU Accountant to conduct verification, periodic physical inventory, analysis, and validation of the existence of those dormant accounts;
b. File the request for authority to write-off dormant unliquidated fund transfers to the COA Audit Team Leaders (ATL) and/or Supervising Auditor (SA), if warranted; and
c. Require the LGU Accountant to make the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts, if write-off was granted.

We encourage and urge the Local Chief Executive to establish sound ecological solid waste management systems and then constitute the Municipal Solid Waste Management (SWM) Boards in pursuant to Section 2 (par. 2), Rule VIII and Section 1, Rule VI of IRR of RA 9003.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive institute the creation of Local School Board (LSB) in his locality and then required the constituted LSB for the preparation of Annual School Board Budget for purposes of proper utilization and disbursement of Special Education Fund (SEF) in compliance with Section 98 of RA 7160 and Item Nos. 4 and 5.2 of DEPED-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017.

1.8 We recommended that the LGU Local Chief Executive direct the LGU
Accountant to secure the monthly bank statement from its authorized
government depository bank (AGDB) for all their bank accounts
maintained and then prepare the corresponding monthly bank
reconciliation statements for each bank account in accordance with Section
74 of PD No. 1445 and Sections 3.1 and 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011.

2.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive of the municipality to:
a. Direct the Local Inventory Committee to continue the conduct of a
physical inventory of all equipment belonging to their respective
offices; and (2) then to submit a complete Annual Physical
Inventory of all property under the agency control as of 31,
December, on General Form (41) not later than 31 January of each
year; and
b. Direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain a subsidiary record
particularly Property Ledger Cards (PLC) for property, plant and
equipment accounts.

3.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should direct the
Municipal Accountant to:
a. Conduct depth review at least at each annual reporting date the
residual value and the useful life of an asset upon recognition of
depreciable amount of each lug’s property, plant and equipment;
b. Take the necessary adjustments noted in the books of accounts by
drawing a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV); and
c. If expectations differ from previous estimates, the change(s) is
accounted for as a change in an accounting estimate in accordance
with IPSAS 3, Accounting Policies, Changes in Accounting
Estimates and Errors.

4.7 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive should enjoin the
compliance with the requirements of the circular by undertaking the
following by his concerned local officials, to wit:
a. Municipal Accountant
i. Prepare the Journal Voucher to record the beginning balance of the
local road network and its components in the general ledger and
the Local Road Network Ledger Card, respectively;
ii. Support the JV with the Registry of Public Infrastructures for
Roads, working paper on the distribution of costs for the road
components, and working paper on the determination of the
depreciated replacement cost for road components with no
available cost per registry;
iii. Keep and maintain subsidiary records to roads and road
components for every road network; and
iv. Prepare a lapsing schedule for the computation of the depreciation
tor each component at the end of the year.
b. General Services Officer
i. Maintain a Local Road Inventory and Road Map; and
ii. Keep a complete Local Road Network Property Card tor all roads
and its Components.
c. Municipal Engineer
i. Provide the local accountant and the general series officer with the
complete description and cost segregation of road components for
road projects.

5.5 We recommended that top management instruct:
a. The Municipal Treasurer to issue a check with complete supporting
documents for the fund transfer of unexpended LDRRM Fund
from General Fund to Special Trust Fund, and
b. The Municipal Account to record the transfer of unexpended
LDRRM Fund by drawing a Journal Entry Voucher taking the
appropriate accounting entries under General and Trust Fund.
i. For General Fund books, debit the account Transfer of Unspent Current Year
DRRM Funds to the Trust Funds (Account Code 5-02-15-010) and credit the
account Cash in Bank – Local Currency Current Account (Account Code 1-01-
02-010).
ii. While for Trust Fund books, debit the account Cash in Bank – Local Currency
Current Account (Account Code 1-01-02-010) and credit the account Trust
Liabilities – Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund (Account Code 2-
04-01-020).

6.4 We recommended that Management direct the Local Disaster Risk
Reduction and Management Officer (LDDRMO) to prepare and submit
Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan
(LDRRMFIP) based on the LDRRM Plan and Budget approved by the
LDDRM Council.

7.3 We recommended that the concerned municipal officials and employees
who are determined as persons responsible/liable settle/comply
immediately with the requirements of the subject of NS/ND/NC.

We recommended that the LGU Local Chief Executive direct the LGU Accountant to secure the monthly bank statement from its authorized government depository bank (AGDB) for all their bank accounts maintained and then prepare the corresponding monthly bank reconciliation statements for each bank account in accordance with Section 74 of PD No. 1445 and Sections 3.1 and 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011.

We recommended that the Management widen the utilization of 20% development fund to the three priority programs and projects such as social development and environment management economic development and environment management.
We recommended that the concerned municipal officials and employees who are determined as persons responsible/liable settle/comply immediately with the requirements of the subject of NS/ND/NC.

8. We recommended  that  the  LGU  Local  Chief  Executive  direct  the  LGU
Accountant to secure the  monthly  bank  statement  from  its  authorized
government depository bank (AGDB) for all their bank accounts  maintained
and then prepare the corresponding monthly bank reconciliation statements
for each bank account in accordance with Section 74 of PD  No.  1445  and
Sections 3.1 and 3.2 of COA Circular No. 96-011.

14. We recommended that LGU Management undertake  the  following  course  of
actions in pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005, to wit:

   a.  Direct  the  LGU  Accountant  to   conduct   regular   and   periodic
      verification, analysis, and  validation  of  the  existence  of  those
      dormant accounts;




   b. File the request for authority to write-off dormant unliquidated  fund
      transfers to the COA  Audit  Team  Leaders  (ATL)  and/or  Supervising
      Auditor (SA), if warranted; and



   c. Require the LGU Accountant to make the necessary  adjustments  in  the
      books of accounts, if write-off was granted.

21. We recommended that the concerned LGU top officials make  the  necessary
step for the complete recognition of Local Road Network in the  books  of
accounts as provided in the said circular, such as:

   a. Conduct an inventory of the local roads under the LGU’s jurisdiction;

   b. Maintain the following  records/reports:  Report  of  the  Local  Road
      Network (RLRN), Report on the Physical Count  of  Local  Road  Network
      (RPCLRN), Local Road Network  Ledger  Card  (LRNLC),  and  Local  Road
      Network Property Card (LRNPC);

   c. Make the necessary adjusting entries in the books upon recognition  of
      Local Road Network account.

25. We recommended that the Management widen the allocation  of  utilization
of 20% development fund to the three priority programs and projects  such
as social development, economic development and environment management.

32. We encourage and urge the Local Chief Executive  to  establish  a  sound
ecological  solid  waste  management  systems  and  then  constitute  the
Municipal Solid Waste Management (SWM) Boards in pursuant  to  Section  2
(par. 2), Rule VIII and Section 1, Rule VI of IRR of RA 9003.

39. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive institute the creation  of
Local  School  Board  (LSB)  in  his  locality  and  then  required   the
constituted LSB for the preparation of Annual  School  Budget  Board  for
purposes of proper utilization and disbursement of Special Education Fund
(SEF) in compliance with Section 98 of RA 7160 and Item Nos. 4 and 5.2 of
DEPED-DBM-DILG JC No. 1, s. 2017.

42. We recommended that the concerned municipal officials and employees  who
are determined as persons  responsible/liable  settle/comply  immediately
with the requirements of the subject of NS/ND/NC.

It is recommended that the top management should initiate necessary reforms for fiscal reporting and accountability by:

demonstrating leadership by example;

testing personnel to gain work competence and/or hiring additional qualified personnel and or restructuring existing personnel giving the right job for the right person; and

enforcing due compliance with laws, rules and regulations on the rendition of financial reports.

It is further recommended that he/they should direct the concerned municipal accountable officer to submit promptly the periodic financial reports together with the pertinent supporting documents to the concerned local auditor so that timely post-audit of transactions could be made and verification of the same could be facilitated. Consequently, they/he could also be informed immediately of any deficiencies/errors detected and corrections /adjustments thereof could be effected to their advantage.

It is recommended that the LGU should include in its annual budget an appropriation for LGU league contributions as a source of fund of the leagues for its continue existence and for carrying out its powers and functions as well as its programs/activities effectively and efficiently. In case there is a disbursement by LGU for such contributions, the LGU should ask for an Official Receipt as proof of receipt of payment of contributions to the league. In the event that there is fund transfer to the league, a liquidation thereof must be demanded by the concerned LGU to the league.

Encourage and urge the Local Chief Executive to establish a sound ecological solid waste management systems and then constitute the Municipal Solid Waste Management (SWM) Boards in pursuant to Section 2 (par. 2), Rule VIII and Section 1, Rule VI of IRR of RA 5447.

It is recommended that the management should widen the allocation of utilization of 20% development fund to the three priority programs and projects such as social development, economic development and environment management.

The local chief executive of the municipality should: direct the Local Inventory Committee to continue the conduct of a physical inventory of all equipment belonging to their respective offices; and (2) then to submit a complete Annual Physical Inventory of all property under the agency control as of 31, December, on General Form (41) not later than 31 January of each year; and direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain a subsidiary records particularly Property Ledger Cards (PLC) for property, plant and equipment accounts.

9. It is recommended that  the  top  management  should  initiate  necessary
   reforms for fiscal reporting and accountability by:

   a. demonstrating leadership by example;
   b. training personnel to gain work competence  and/or  hiring  additional
      qualified personnel and or restructuring existing personnel giving the
      right job for the right person; and
   c. enforcing due compliance with  laws,  rules  and  regulations  on  the
      rendition of financial reports.

10. It is further recommended  that  he/they  should  direct  the  concerned
   municipal accountable officer to submit promptly the  periodic  financial
   reports together with the pertinent supporting documents to the concerned
   local auditor so that timely post-audit of transactions could be made and
   verification of the same  could  be  facilitated.  Consequently,  they/he
   could also be informed immediately of  any  deficiencies/errors  detected
   and  corrections  /adjustments  thereof  could  be  effected   to   their
   advantage.

14. It is recommended that the LGU should include in its  annual  budget  an
   appropriation for LGU league contributions as a source  of  fund  of  the
   leagues for its continue existence and for carrying out  its  powers  and
   functions as well as its programs/activities effectively and efficiently.
   In case there is a disbursement by LGU for such  contributions,  the  LGU
   should ask for an Official Receipt as proof  of  receipt  of  payment  of
   contributions to the league. In the event that there is fund transfer  to
   the league, a liquidation thereof must be demanded by the  concerned  LGU
   to the league.

20. Encourage and urge the  Local  Chief  Executive  to  establish  a  sound
   ecological  solid  waste  management  systems  and  then  constitute  the
   Municipal Solid Waste Management (SWM) Boards in pursuant  to  Section  2
   (par. 2), Rule VIII and Section 1, Rule VI of IRR of RA 5447.

23. It is recommended that the management should  widen  the  allocation  of
   utilization of 20% development fund to the three  priority  programs  and
   projects such as social development, economic development and environment
   management.

31. The local chief executive of the municipality should:

   a. direct the Local Inventory Committee to  continue  the  conduct  of  a
      physical inventory of all  equipment  belonging  to  their  respective
      offices; and (2) then to submit a complete Annual  Physical  Inventory
      of all property under the  agency  control  as  of  31,  December,  on
      General Form (41) not later than 31 January of each year; and



b.  direct  the  Municipal  Accountant  to  maintain  a  subsidiary  records
   particularly  Property  Ledger  Cards  (PLC)  for  property,  plant   and
   equipment accounts.

33. It is recommended that the concerned municipal officials  and  employees
   who are  determined  as  persons  responsible/liable  should  immediately
   settle/comply with the requirements of the subject of NS/ND/NC.

The local chief executive of the municipality should:       

create an Inventory Committee: which composed of the representative of the Chief Executive, the Treasurer and Property or Supply Officer or Accountable Officer as members; and whose functions are: (1) to conduct a physical inventory of all equipment belonging to their respective offices at least once a year, to be witnessed by representative of the agency auditor; and (2) to submit an Annual Physical Inventory of all property under the agency control as of 31, December, on General Form (41) not later than 31 January of each year; and                 

direct the Municipal Accountant to maintain a subsidiary records particularly Property Ledger Cards (PLC) for property, plant and equipment accounts.

It is recommended that the management should initiate measures designed to come up with a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) with the GSIS defining therein the agreed balance of payables to GSIS, if any, and the terms and conditions in the settlement thereof.  The following steps may help in facilitating reconciliation of records, to wit: Secure detailed profile of payables per GSIS records; Reconstruct old GSIS accounts, if necessary, and update records; Reconstruct old personnel profile, if necessary, and update personnel profile; Counter-check GSIS records with the reconstructed and updated accounting and personnel records of the LGU; Negotiate with the GSIS for any differences that may be noted during counter-checking activity; Enter into a formal agreement with the GSIS in the form of a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA); and Appropriate fund for the settlement of any payables to the GSIS as agreed in the MOA.

We recommend that management should conduct a thorough inquiry on the circumstances surrounding the existence of those dormant accounts, and their proper disposition as prescribed under existing regulations.

We recommend that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant, and the Municipal Treasurer to: faithfully observe Section 4 (6 & 7) of Presidential Decree No. 1445 (PD 1445) and Section 28 of National Government Accounting System (NGAS), Volume I and other applicable laws, rules and regulations in the processing of claim against government funds; and cause the immediate submission of all supporting papers required in each of the transactions paid

Required the Municipal Treasurer to strictly observe the issuance of official receipt to each payor as proof of acknowledgement of receipt of payment and the preparation of report of collection thereof that would show the details of payors in compliance with the Section 68 paragraph 1 of PD 1445 and Sections 16, 22 and 23 of NGAS, Volume I.

Cause for the conduct orientation among concerned personnel on GAD in particular to the provisions of PCW-NEDA-DBM JC 2012-01. Specifically, make sure that proper budgeting, accounting and reporting of GAD Fund are duly complied with and, that, the fund can only be utilized according to the intended purpose(s).
